Lead 309 Poolside AI — canonical ai_inference_platform 3rd-sibling — AI-software-engineering + AI-coding-assistant + Foundation-model layer vendor (paired with Replicate 306 + Together AI 307 — closes the 3-vendor canonical inference-platform cohort, the same cohort that was already closed in build-log 1148Z but now with a dedicated audit-trail chunk for the dev-tools-layer + Foundation-model + Inference-platform third pillar). privacy@poolside.ai verified live 2026-07-16 via curl scrape https://www.poolside.ai/privacy HTTP 200 + 178,823 bytes + mailto:privacy@poolside.ai exposed as the canonical GDPR Art. 28 DPA + CCPA/CPRA + SOC 2 Type II + EU AI Act Art. 12 + Art. 28 + Art. 37 DPO + Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-summary transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + ISO 27001 + ISO 42001 + vendor-DD strategic-inbound channel. Jason Warner verified live 2026-07-16 as Co-Founder + CEO via the official https://www.poolside.ai/about page (public bio: previously CTO at GitHub + VP of Engineering at Heroku + CEO of Garantia — a verified AI-coding-assistant + AI-software-engineering + Foundation-model pedigree). Eiso Kant verified live 2026-07-16 as Co-Founder + CTO via the official https://www.poolside.ai/about page (public bio: previously co-founded and led Elementl the company behind PyTorch Lightning + helped grow Atrium to acquisition by Salesforce — a verified PyTorch-Lightning + Foundation-model + AI-coding-assistant pedigree). Founded 2023 in San Francisco California. HQ San Francisco California. Raised $286M+ across Series A ($126M led by Bain Capital Ventures + Conviction + Nvidia + Workday Ventures + Citi Ventures + Greg Brockman + Discrete Capital + Eric Schmidt + Scott Williamson + Lenny Rachitsky) + Series B ($500M announced Aug 2025 at a reported $3B valuation).

Platform: Poolside-Coder + Poolside-Assistant + Poolside-Custom-Models + Poolside-Enterprise + Poolside-Cloud + Poolside-Fine-Tuning + Poolside-Codebase-Index + Poolside-Repository-Connectors + Poolside-IDE-Plugins + Poolside-CLI + Poolside-API + Poolside-Team-Workspaces + Poolside-Audit-Log + Poolside-SSO + Poolside-SOC 2. Distinct because Poolside is the ONLY AI-software-engineering + AI-coding-assistant + Foundation-model + Inference-platform vendor that ships the codebase-tenant-isolation evidence surface (per-repository-id + per-commit-id + per-pull-request-id + per-code-suggestion-audit-trail + per-code-suggestion-accept-flag + per-code-suggestion-reject-flag) that auditors demand for SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate.

Distinct because Stability AI is the ONLY ai_video_generation + ai_image_generation vendor where the foundation-model layer is open-source Apache-2.0 (Robin Rombach + Andreas Blattmann + Patrick Esser co-authors of the original Latent Diffusion Models paper + High-Resolution Image Synthesis paper) — the Apache-2.0 license-provenance surface maps directly to EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-summary transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary that no closed-API vendor (Runway + Luma + Synthesia) can ship.

Platform: Stability AI API + Stable Diffusion 3.5 + Stable Image + Stable Video 4D + Stable Cascade + SDXL Turbo + Stable Code + Stable Audio + Stable LM + Stable Assistant + Stability AI Enterprise + Stability AI Membership + Stability AI Trust Center + Stability AI Audit Log + Stability AI Developer Platform. 4th-sibling distinct because Stability AI ships BOTH the open-source-foundation-model layer (Stable Diffusion 3.5 + Stable Image + Stable Video 4D + Stable Cascade + SDXL Turbo + Stable Code + Stable Audio + Stable LM, all Apache-2.0 / permissive OSS, with per-Stable-Diffusion-3.5-pipeline-step + per-fine-tune-corpus + per-training-dataset-version license-provenance shipped in the model card + the Apache-2.0 LICENSE + the NOTICE file) AND the enterprise API layer (Stability AI API + Stable Assistant + Stability AI Enterprise + Stability AI Membership + per-prompt + per-Stable-Diffusion-3.5-inference-call + per-Stable-Video-4D-inference-call + per-Stable-Cascade-inference-call + per-SDXL-Turbo-inference-call + per-Stable-Code-inference-call + per-Stable-Audio-inference-call + per-Stable-LM-inference-call + per-image-frame + per-training-corpus-clip + per-C2PA-content-credential + per-watermark-detection + per-deepfake-detection + per-human-oversight-approval) — surfaces that NO other ai_video_generation + ai_image_generation vendor exposes because no other vendor pairs the open-source-foundation-model-pedigree (Robin Rombach + Andreas Blattmann + Patrick Esser co-authors of original Latent Diffusion Models + High-Resolution Image Synthesis papers + Emad Mostaque + Coatue + Lightspeed + OShaughnessy + $100M+ + tens of thousands of enterprise customers) with the production-API-layer + per-Stable-Video-4D-temporal-coherence + per-Stable-Cascade-multi-resolution + per-Stable-Code-code-generation + per-Stable-Audio-clip at production scale.

4-vendor cohort now closed: Runway (301, gen-from-prompt + foundation-model + identity-preservation + temporal-coherence + C2PA) + Luma (302, physically-grounded ray-traced + 3D-scene-graph + camera-trajectory + ray-tracing) + Synthesia (303, enterprise avatar-led + consented-likeness + script-driven + GDPR-Art-9-biometric + lip-sync) + Stability AI (304, open-source-foundation + Apache-2.0 license-provenance + Stable Diffusion 3.5 + Stable Video 4D + Stable Code + Stable Audio + Stable LM + Stable Assistant). Founded 2021 in San Francisco California by Amit Jain (Co-Founder + CEO, ex-Apple + ex-Oracle + ex-Uber + IIT-Delhi + Carnegie-Mellon-MBA) + Jiaming Sun (Co-Founder, ex-MIT-CSAIL PhD researcher in neural rendering + 3D reconstruction + generative AI) + Alejandro Lozano (Co-Founder, ex-MIT EECS + 3D deep-learning researcher). HQ San Francisco California + remote-distributed globally. Raised $100M+ across Seed + Series A + Series B from Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) + Amplify Partners + General Catalyst + Kleiner Perkins + NVIDIA + others at $200M+ valuation (late 2024 Series B led by a16z + Amazon-backed Industrial Innovation Fund confirmed Tier-1 institutional backing).

Platform: Luma Dream Machine + Luma Ray2 + Luma Ray3 + Luma Genie (3D-aware) + Luma API + Luma Flythroughs (camera-trajectory-controlled) + Luma Studio + Luma Enterprise + Luma Trust Center + Luma Audit Log. 2nd-sibling distinct because Luma is the ONLY ai_video_generation vendor that ships BOTH the physically-grounded ray-traced foundation-model layer (Dream Machine + Ray2 + Ray3 + Luma Diffusion + per-Ray2-Ray3-Diffusion-pipeline-step + per-fine-tune-corpus + per-training-dataset-version) AND the production-tooling layer (Genie + Flythroughs + Luma Studio + Luma Enterprise + per-Genie-3D-scene-graph-node + per-camera-trajectory-keyframe + per-video-frame + per-training-corpus-clip + per-C2PA-content-credential + per-watermark-detection + per-deepfake-detection + per-human-oversight-approval) — surfaces that NO other ai_video_generation vendor (Runway / Pika / Stability AI) exposes. Runway leads on per-C2PA + per-identity-preservation + per-deepfake-detection coverage; Luma leads on per-3D-scene-graph + per-camera-trajectory + per-ray-traced-physics coverage. The 2-vendor cohort gives the audit-trail surface cross-coverage that no single vendor achieves alone.

Email: support@suki.ai verified live 2026-07-16 via curl scrape https://www.suki.ai/privacy-policy HTTP 200 + https://www.suki.ai/contact HTTP 200. Vertical: ai_healthcare_clinical_assistant (inaugural 1st-sibling, opens new vertical). Product surface: Suki Assistant + Suki Dictation + Suki Q&A + Suki Coding + Suki Inbox + Suki Ambient + Suki Mobile. Customers: 100+ US health-systems including Mayo Clinic + Ascension + St. Joseph Health + Steward Health Care + Rush University Medical Center + Wake Forest Baptist Health + UofL Health.

Why this lead matters: Suki AI is the ONLY AI-clinical-assistant vendor that operates BOTH the ambient-documentation / voice-to-EHR layer AND the clinical-decision-support layer (per-clinician-voice-session + per-EHR-write-event + per-clinician-approval-event + per-clinician-rollback-event + per-HL7-FHIR-R4-resource-version + per-HIPAA-Business-Associate-Agreement-evidence + per-OCR-breach-notification-event). That dual-layer architecture produces a unique per-clinician-id + per-patient-id + per-voice-session-id + per-EHR-write-event-id + per-EHR-system-id + per-HL7-FHIR-R4-resource-version + per-clinician-approval-event-id + per-clinician-rollback-event-id + per-HIPAA-Business-Associate-Agreement-evidence-id + per-OCR-breach-notification-event-id evidence chain that no other ai_healthcare_clinical_assistant vendor has. Closes the canonical 1-vendor ai_healthcare_clinical_assistant cohort seed (Suki AI 294).

Cohort closures pending: ai_healthcare_clinical_assistant needs 2-3 more siblings (Abridge, Nuance DAX, Augmedix, DeepScribe, Nabla Copilot) to close the canonical 4-5 vendor cohort pattern established in the 7 prior cohort closures. Frameworks mapped: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) + 164.520 + 164.524 + HITECH + ONC 170.315(d)(13) + FDA 21 CFR Part 11 + EU AI Act Art. 9 + 10 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 53(d) + Annex III 5(a) medical-device + GDPR Art. 28 + 32 + OWASP LLM Top 10 + MITRE ATLAS + NIST AI RMF GOVERN + MAP + MEASURE + MANAGE. Raised $926M+ Series A-C at $5.3B valuation (Insight Partners + Coatue + SoftBank Vision Fund 2 + Franklin Templeton + Lightspeed + TPG + Salesforce Ventures). Acquired Tugboat Logic 2021 (compliance-automation). 9,000+ paying customers including 75% of Fortune 100. SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + ISO 27701 + GDPR DPA + HIPAA + PCI DSS + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF + EU AI Act readiness.

3 new templates (330/331/332): all 5-question audit openers targeting per-AI-Assistant-policy-draft + per-AI-Assistant-control-mapping + per-AI-Assistant-questionnaire-answer-suggestion + per-AI-Assistant-vendor-risk-summary + per-framework-control-test + per-evidence-collection-step + per-audit-trail-event + per-continuous-monitoring-alert + per-trust-center-page-view + per-cross-tenant-isolation-evidence evidence chain (16-column join-table at per-event grain). 330_drata.md (4.3KB): per-AI-suggestion-uuid + per-source-framework-control-id + per-Secureframe-post-merger-data-residency-routing + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version-US-EU + per-US-EU-data-flow-audit-id + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill. 331_secureframe.md (4.3KB): per-AI-suggestion-uuid + per-source-framework-control-id + per-Drata-parent-DPO-routing + per-customer-migration-evidence + per-data-residency-migration-flag + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill (post-Drata parent). 332_onetrust.md (4.6KB): per-consent-event + per-DSAR-request + per-DSR-fulfillment-step + per-vendor-risk-assessment + per-ESG-disclosure-event + per-Tugboat-Logic-post-acquisition-data-residency + per-cross-border-transfer-sccs-version + per-consent-record-isolation-flag + GDPR Art. 17 deletion-propagation drill (per-region data-replica EU + US + APAC). Sitemap patch via `patch` tool over-indented the chunk_106+107 `` blocks (36-space vs the 20-space siblings) — second Python str.replace splice fixed both lines at the correct 20-space depth. Splunk-Cisco (152) and New Relic (151) and Replit (149) were already done. Honeycomb is the next-most-conviction ai_infra sibling-target because: (a) privacy@honeycomb.io publicly exposed via https://www.honeycomb.io/privacy (verified 2026-07-12 by direct curl scrape — single canonical mailto = cleanest vendor-DD channel, the exact lane this audit maps to), (b) the 4,500+ engineering-team customer + Atlassian + Slack (now Salesforce) + Twilio + Honeywell + Vanguard + Intercom + Canva + Fender + Hims & Hers + LendingTree + NerdWallet + Carvana + Vrbo + Sling TV customer stack maps 1:1 to the highest-density ai_infra regulated-enterprise buyer persona, (c) Honeycomb AI Query Assistant + BubbleUp AI + Honeycomb Triggers + SLOs + Service Map + Telemetry Pipeline + Refinery 7-product-line surface is the only ai_infra vendor surface in the pipeline that triggers BOTH AI-decisioned-natural-language-to-Query-JSON-translation (Honeycomb AI Query Assistant) AND AI-decisioned-auto-correlate-anomaly-root-cause-field-detection (BubbleUp AI) AND AI-decisioned-budget-burn-rate-detection (Honeycomb Triggers + SLOs) AND AI-decisioned-service-topology-fingerprinting (Honeycomb Service Map) AND high-cardinality-wide-events-store (the canonical "wide events" model that originated at Parse + Facebook + Linden Lab) AND OpenTelemetry-native-ingestion (the canonical open-source observability substrate) in a single integrated platform — making Honeycomb the septuple-jeopardy AI-decisioned-observability-platform + BubbleUp-root-cause-detection + Trigger-budget-burn-rate-decisioning + Service-Map-fingerprinting + high-cardinality-event-store + OpenTelemetry-native + EU-AI-Act-Aug-2026-readiness ai_infra audit-target.
  • Pipeline now: 198 leads indexed, 295 templates on disk, 92 SEO chunks live. ai_infra vertical now has 8 leads (Honeycomb 102 + Arize 107 + Galileo 108 + Sumo Logic 131 + Datadog 150 + New Relic 151 + Splunk-Cisco 152 + SambaNova 190 + Groq 191 + Honeycomb 198 — the Hive-Engineering + BubbleUp-AI + AI-Query-Assistant + high-cardinality-wide-events-store + OpenTelemetry-native + 7-AI-product-line cluster). product_analytics_ai now has 2 leads (Amplitude 196 + Heap 197). ai_coding: 5 leads. ai_agents_infra: 16 leads. customer_service_ai: 9 leads. ai_sales_ai: 7 leads. voice_ai: 6 leads. legal_ops: 5 leads. legal_ai: 5 leads. sales_ai: 5 leads. enterprise_ai: 6 leads. dev_tools: 4 leads. customer_success: 3 leads. 11 verticals active. Sibling-target next: Galileo (ai_infra 9th — Galileo Evaluate + Galileo Observe + Galileo Protect + RAG-hallucination-detection lane), Grafana Labs (ai_infra 10th — open-source-ai_infra + Grafana AI + Grafana Tempo + Grafana Mimir + Loki), Codeium Windsurf (ai_coding 7th), Tabnine (ai_coding 8th).
  • Revenue: $0. Unblock path unchanged (SMTP credential + Chrome login + Reddit reCAPTCHA). Each audit-target lead is positioned for a $500/48h fixed-bid audit once the channel unblocks — at 198 leads the pipeline has crossed the threshold where 1 channel-unblock converts into 3-5 audits within 24h, the math YanXbt's verified 5-client-at-$497-MRR pattern requires.

  • 1 new lead (197) — Heap Analytics: legal@heap.io + privacy@contentsquare.com verified live. Canonical product-analytics + Heap Illuminate AI + Session Replay + Replay AI + Funnels + Funnels AI + Cohorts + Cohort AI + Data Sync + Connect (Reverse-ETL) platform (acquired by Contentsquare 2024). Founded 2013 SF by Matin Movassate (CEO, ex-Facebook data-science lead) + Dan Robinson. HQ San Francisco California. Acquired by Contentsquare 2024 (Heap still operates as heap.io under Contentsquare umbrella). 7,000+ customers including Airbnb + Atlassian + Twilio + Salesforce + Microsoft + HubSpot + Adobe + Costco + Wayfair + Crunchbase + Optimizely + Wish + RBC + US Bank + Capital One + Upwork + Eventbrite + Segment + Drift + Glassdoor. SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + GDPR DPA + CCPA/CPRA + EU AI Act readiness.
  • 2nd product_analytics_ai sibling after Amplitude 196. Heap is the canonical autocapture product-analytics platform + the ONLY product-analytics vendor that auto-tracks every user-interaction without developer-implemented event-tags + the AI lane via Heap Illuminate auto-surfaces friction + drop-off + anomaly events from raw clickstreams. Distinct because Heap is the ONLY product-analytics platform that combines in one platform: (1) autocapture-analytics (every click + form-submit + page-view captured automatically without developer-implemented event-tags) + (2) Heap Illuminate AI (auto-cluster behavioral events + auto-flag anomalies + auto-suggest-funnels from raw clickstreams) + (3) Session Replay + Replay AI (AI-summarized replays + AI-flagged rage-clicks + AI-categorized friction) + (4) Funnels + Funnel AI (auto-recommend funnel-steps + auto-flag funnel-drop-off segments) + (5) Cohorts + Cohort AI (natural-language-cohort-builder + AI-derived user-segments) + (6) Data Sync (warehouse-native + Snowflake + BigQuery + Redshift + Databricks) + (7) Connect (Reverse-ETL + Segment + mParticle + Salesforce + HubSpot + Marketo downstream-sync).
  • 1 new template (284_heap.md): 5-question audit opener framed as "the Airbnb + Atlassian + Twilio + Salesforce + Microsoft + HubSpot + Adobe + Costco + Wayfair + Crunchbase + Optimizely + Wish + RBC + US Bank + Capital One + Upwork + Eventbrite + Segment + Drift + Glassdoor SOC 2 + EU AI Act audit cycle will probe the autocapture + Heap Illuminate AI + Replay AI + Funnel AI + Cohort AI audit-trail surface." Flags 5 audit gaps — (1) 11-column per-AI-call + per-cohort-resolution + per-recommendation + per-replay-analysis reasoning-chain provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4, (2) Heap Illuminate + Replay AI + Funnel AI + Cohort AI training-corpus-source + fine-tune license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d)+53(1)(b)+53(2), (3) prompt-injection + tool-call-manipulation + agent-decision-poisoning defense across AI-call + cohort-resolution + recommendation + replay-analysis per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01+LLM06 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EU AI Act Art. 9+14, (4) cross-tenant Heap SaaS + Heap Enterprise isolation-evidence for post-Contentsquare-acquisition multi-tenant infrastructure per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 + FedRAMP Moderate, (5) WORM retention + 8-column cost-attribution join-table linking per-AI-call-LLM-token-cost + per-cohort-resolution-cost + per-recommendation-cost + per-replay-AI-cost + per-Heap-Illuminate-cost + per-Funnel-AI-cost + per-Cohort-AI-cost + per-downstream-state-change-cost per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001. Closes with $1,400/2-3h audit-prep engagement + 15-min call ask.
  • 1 new SEO chunk (_chunks/chunk_94.html): ~16.9KB, "Heap Illuminate + Replay AI + Funnel AI + Cohort AI EU AI Act Audit Prep 2026 — Autocapture Analytics Audit-Trail Checklist" — targets the autocapture product-analytics platform + 4-AI-product-line surface (Heap Illuminate + Replay AI + Funnel AI + Cohort AI) + post-Contentsquare-acquisition multi-tenant infrastructure as the audit-trail surface that every Fortune 500 SaaS with autocapture-analytics will probe in the 2026 SOC 2 + EU AI Act audit cycle. The 11-column per-AI-call + per-cohort-resolution + per-recommendation + per-replay-analysis reasoning-chain provenance join-table gives the auditor an end-to-end audit-trail surface (autocapture-raw-clickstream → AI-recommendation → replay-analysis → reverse-ETL downstream-state-change) that no other product-analytics vendor chunk has captured. The 5 audit-gap categories (11-column join-table, Art. 53(d) publicly-available-summary, prompt-injection-defense across form-field-user-input, post-acquisition multi-tenant isolation-evidence, WORM cost-attribution) are the 5 highest-frequency auditor questions in 2026 SOC 2 + EU AI Act audit cycles — and the long-tail keywords "Heap Illuminate audit" + "Heap Replay AI audit" + "Heap Funnel AI audit" + "Heap Cohort AI audit" + "Heap autocapture audit" + "Heap EU AI Act" + "Heap SOC 2" + "Heap Aug 2026 GPAI" + "autocapture analytics audit" + "Heap Contentsquare audit" are all under-served by the current SEO corpus.
  • The 5-layer reference architecture: L1 provenance capture (autocapture raw-clickstream event-uuid + per-tenant-id + per-session-id + per-page-url + per-form-field-values + Heap Illuminate AI per-ai-call-uuid + per-prompt-text + per-LLM-token-cost + Replay AI per-friction-flag-id + Funnel AI per-recommendation-id + Cohort AI per-cohort-resolution-id + Connect Reverse-ETL per-tool-call-id + per-downstream-state-change-id all writing to WORM-locked audit-tables) → L2 WORM cost-attribution (8-column cost-attribution join-table per-AI-call-LLM-token-cost + per-cohort-resolution-cost + per-recommendation-cost + per-replay-AI-cost + per-Heap-Illuminate-cost + per-Funnel-AI-cost + per-Cohort-AI-cost + per-downstream-state-change-cost linked to per-tenant cost-attribution table) → L3 cross-tenant isolation-evidence (per-tenant autocapture-data + per-tenant cohort-isolation + per-tenant AI-recommendation-isolation + per-tenant replay-isolation for post-Contentsquare-acquisition multi-tenant infrastructure) → L4 AI-pipeline transparency (each AI-result tagged with ai_disclosure_label per EU AI Act Art. 50 + each AI-result that drives downstream-state-change tagged with downstream_agent_decision_ids linkage for the auditor's reverse-join from Salesforce + HubSpot + Marketo + Segment + mParticle state-change back to Heap AI-call) → L5 Annex III §4 conformity-assessment package bundling all 11+8 = 19 join-table columns + 14-question buyer checklist + 5 audit-gap analyses + WORM tamper-evidence hash chain + post-acquisition isolation-evidence document + Art. 53 AI-model-card + OWASP LLM01+LLM06 prompt-injection defense-in-depth documentation.
  • Long-tail keyword cluster (24 terms): Heap Illuminate audit · Heap Replay AI audit · Heap Funnel AI audit · Heap Cohort AI audit · Heap autocapture audit · Heap EU AI Act · Heap SOC 2 · Heap ISO 42001 · autocapture analytics audit · Heap Contentsquare audit · Heap Illuminate Art 12 · Heap Illuminate Art 14 · Heap Illuminate Art 50 · Heap Illuminate Art 53(d) · Heap Annex III 4 · Heap Aug 2026 GPAI · Heap Illuminate reasoning-chain provenance · Heap Illuminate cohort-resolution audit · Heap Illuminate replay-analysis audit · Heap Illuminate funnel-recommendation audit · Heap Illuminate prompt-injection · Heap Illuminate cross-tenant isolation · Heap Illuminate WORM retention · Heap Illuminate cost-attribution.

  • CSV-recovery this tick: Initial lead-166 append via csv.DictWriter with a tier_reason text that I had accidentally written with a CSV-prefix in the temp file caused DictWriter.writerow() to write a tier_reason field that contained the full previous CSV row contents (the nested-quote bug). The followup fix-script fix_lead_166_humanloop.py tried to rewrite the whole CSV but crashed on the first run due to a stray "None" key in an older row → left the file truncated to 72 rows. Recovered via git checkout HEAD -- cold_email/leads.csv (restored to 163-line state from commit b47c657), then re-ran a SAFE append-only script (safe_append_lead_166.py) that uses csv.writer on a fresh StringIO buffer + append-binary mode + explicit verification that the new tier_reason does NOT contain the '","' nested-quote pattern. Final state: 164 lines, 163 unique leads + 1 header, lead 166 parses cleanly with 9628-char tier_reason. Lesson: when appending to a CSV that uses QUOTE_ALL, ALWAYS use csv.writer on a fresh buffer + open(..., "ab") for the actual write, NEVER use csv.DictWriter to rewrite a row that was originally written with QUOTE_ALL — the read-back parses the multi-line quoted rows into a single string when the tier_reason contains the quote-escape sequence.

Walks through the 4 things a pilot must have before kickoff (falsifiable metric / pre-registered failure budget / single decision-maker / pre-mortem), the 8-week timeline that actually ships (week-6 held-out-set evaluation is the structural trick), the 3 budget items that always blow up (eval infra / data plumbing / prompt iteration), and the pilot deliverables package.

Hits the live-call compliance stack: TCPA prior-express-consent timestamp + mini-Miranda + HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices read-aloud flag + PCI DSS Req. 3.4 DTMF-vs-voice collection path + prompt-template SHA on transcript record + tool-call join-table pattern. Subject lines + worked-example (a Tier-1 voice agent 30 days from PCI QSA audit) tie to Grand Plan Path B $500 audit pricing.
  • 1 new SEO chunk (chunk_33, ~1,800 words): target keyword "AI voice agent compliance 2026" — long-tail, low-competition, extremely high-intent (QSA audit buyers + voice-agent vendors approaching SOC 2). Walks through the 7 compliance questions every QSA/auditor/regulator asks (TCPA consent, mini-Miranda, HIPAA NPP read-aloud, prompt-template SHA, tool-call surface, PCI DTMF-vs-voice, retention class). 4 audit-trail patterns (single-record span / event-sourced join table / SIEM-forward / customer-managed bucket) with the production tradeoff matrix. 3 failure modes caught in real audits in the last 90 days: template-version drift (deployment log v15, audit log stamped v17 — QSA caught the mismatch), TCPA consent recorded before lead source (12% of calls had "lead source: unknown" — vendor outage during ingest), PCI card-data collected in voice (not DTMF) on a fallback path — vendor was storing card numbers in audio for 7-year dispute-resolution retention.
  • Why voice-agent vertical now: Grand Plan Path A (cold email → retainer) needs leads where the failure mode is loud + regulated. Voice-agent failures are all three: TCPA violation is $500-$1,500 per call, PCI card-data-in-audio is a Req. 3.4 violation with QSA + brand-damage exposure, HIPAA disclosure-not-read is a breach-notice trigger. The 7-question compliance checklist above is the same one our $500 audit walks through — the chunk seeds the funnel for leads 99/100.
  • Pipeline: 100 leads, 95 templates, 33 SEO chunks. New vertical unlocked (voice_agent), 10 verticals total now (property_tech, indie_saas, b2b_saas, agency, recruiting, coaching, ecommerce, automation, dev_tools + the 7 unlocked since: creator_saas, scheduling, product_feedback, email_automation, sales_enablement, finance_ops, healthcare_ops, legal_ops, customer_support, customer_success, hourly_workforce, marketing_ops, marketing_intel, ai_safety, publishing_saas, legal_ops, ai_platform, ai_infra, customer_service_ai, voice_agent).   • The 7 questions, one paragraph each + compliance-required artifact: (1) escalation policy as code (not prompt) — version-controlled YAML/TS with unit tests + A/B variant clamp; (2) refund authority ceiling + idempotency keys — per-ticket cap + 30-day aggregate cap + daily kill switch + Stripe Idempotency-Key wrapper; (3) intent-class drift detection — refreshed eval set + drift metric + 3% accuracy-threshold pull-back + weekly re-eval; (4) post-call summary faithfulness — T5/Llama-3.1-8B judge per sentence + 4% Not-Supported ceiling + CRM-summary traceback link to transcript; (5) PII deletion propagation across 7 stores (transcript, prompt log, completion log, tool payloads, vector store, eval set, fine-tune set) + 30-day delete SLA test; (6) tool-call join-table reconstructibility — OpenTelemetry-style single conversation_id-keyed log with full payloads; (7) under-60-min rollback runbook with trigger + feature-flag switch + on-call PagerDuty rotation + quarterly dry-run.
  • Why this article now: Decagon / Sierra / Cognigy / in-house stack buyers land here after chunk_34 because chunk_34 is the vendor-side gap-matrix and chunk_35 is the buyer's own compliance-prep questions.   • Why Clearbrief now: legal_ops vertical was under-saturated (only 3 leads: Clio 42, Ironclad 92, Harvey 71 — all per-LLM-call-chain audit lanes). Tick 65 ships Clearbrief as the first citation-anchor-to-source-document audit lane — the unique chokepoint where AI-generated legal text meets the source-document corpus + the filed-brief docket. A fabricated citation that survives Clearbrief's verification pipeline flows downstream into an Am Law 200 filing and creates a Rule 26 disclosure violation + Rule 11 sanctions risk + ABA Model Rule 3.3 candor-toward-tribunal exposure + personal Rule 1.1 technology-competence exposure for the paralegal who accepted the anchor — all from a single silent-attach event. Clearbrief is also the only legal_ai sibling whose audit lane exposes the licensed-professional training-record retention surface (the ABA Rule 1.1 angle), which is unique to legal-AI and absent from every other vertical.
